name: SuperPawn
description_short: SuperPawn North Las Vegas, Nevada — FirstCash is the leading international operator of pawn stores with 3,300+ locations across the U.S., UK, and Latin ...
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the leading international pawn store chain with over 3,300 retail locations across 29 U.S. states, the District of Columbia, the United Kingdom, and Latin America (Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador). The company employs approximately 22,000 people and is indexed in both the S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000, indicating its scale as a publicly traded enterprise in the consumer lending space.

FirstCash's core business model centers on serving cash and credit-constrained consumers through pawn loans—non-recourse loans secured by pledged personal property. The company buys and sells a diverse range of merchandise including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments. Beyond pawn loans, FirstCash offers layaway services (with 10% down payments), gold and precious metals buying, and retail merchandise sales. Through its wholly owned subsidiary AFF, the company also operates a lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions network spanning over 15,000 merchant partner locations nationwide.

FirstCash distinguishes itself through its massive scale and geographic diversification across international markets, which reduces dependence on any single U.S. region. The company's dual focus on pawn lending and technology-driven payment solutions (through AFF) creates multiple revenue streams. Its inclusion in major indices reflects institutional credibility and financial stability, setting it apart from smaller, independent pawn operators. The extensive store network provides genuine convenience for customers across multiple countries.

However, as a pawn lender, FirstCash operates in a sector inherently designed around short-term, high-cost lending secured by collateral. While pawn loans are non-recourse (meaning failure to repay doesn't result in legal action), they require surrendering valuable personal property. The company's profitability depends partly on merchandise markups and forfeited collateral, creating misaligned incentives. Customers with limited financial options may face difficulty retrieving pledged items if unable to repay within loan terms.
address: 6424 Losee Rd #135, North Las Vegas, NV 89086

company_info.headquarters: 6424 Losee Rd #135, North Las Vegas, NV 89086

pros: 3,300+ store locations across 29 U.S. states plus DC, UK, and Latin America—exceptional accessibility and convenience
No credit check or credit history requirement for pawn loans, serving credit-constrained consumers
Non-recourse loans—borrowers cannot be sued or face legal action if unable to repay; only collateral is forfeited
Fast cash access on-site; immediate payment for items sold or pledged
Diverse merchandise buying—accepts jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, instruments, and more
Layaway service with low 10% down payment, providing alternative to immediate full payment
Gold and precious metals buying service for quick liquidity conversion
S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000 indexed company—publicly traded with transparent financial reporting
cons: Pawn loans charge interest and fees that can be high relative to traditional lending; exact rates not disclosed on primary website
Surrendering personal property as collateral means losing access to items until loan is repaid in full—high financial and emotional risk
Merchandise markups on retail sales are substantial; customers often pay significantly more than market value for resold items
Complexity in loan terms and redemption windows may disadvantage consumers with limited financial literacy
Business model profits from customer defaults (forfeited collateral) and merchandise sales, creating potential misalignment with borrower interests
best_for: Cash-constrained consumers needing immediate funds without credit checks or lengthy approval processes
Individuals with valuable personal property (jewelry, electronics, instruments) willing to pledge collateral for short-term loans
People seeking alternative to payday loans or title loans who have acceptable collateral available
Consumers looking to buy used merchandise at retail locations or use layaway services for installment purchases
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for cash-constrained consumers who own valuable personal property and need immediate funds without credit approval—particularly those unable to qualify for traditional personal loans or credit products. The critical caveat is that pawn lending inherently requires surrendering collateral you may value, and the business model profits when customers default; carefully evaluate whether a pawn loan's terms align with your ability to repay and your comfort with losing pledged items.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property
Silver and platinum buying and selling
Retail merchandise sales (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments)
Layaway service with 10% down payment option
Lease-to-own payment solutions (via AFF subsidiary through 15,000+ merchant partners)
